content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case starts with a line containing two integers N and M — the number of vertices and edges. The next M lines each contain two integers u and v denoting a directed edge from u to v. Vertices are numbered from 1 to N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line. If the graph is a DAG, print N space‑separated integers representing a topological ordering. If the graph contains a cycle, print -1.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The graph is a DAG. Kahn's algorithm processes vertices 1,2,3,4,5 in that order, yielding a valid topological ordering.
All edges respect the order 1→2→3→4, so the algorithm outputs the natural ordering.
A linear extension respecting all dependencies is 1 2 3 4 5 6.

solution.approach: We use Kahn's algorithm (BFS based topological sort). First compute the in‑degree of every vertex. All vertices with in‑degree 0 are placed in a queue. Repeatedly remove a vertex from the queue, add it to the answer and decrease the in‑degree of its outgoing neighbours; any neighbour whose in‑degree becomes 0 is enqueued. If we process all N vertices we obtain a valid topological order; otherwise the graph contains a cycle and we output -1. The algorithm runs in O(N+M) time and uses O(N+M) memory.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N + M) – adjacency list, indegree array and queue

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringBuilder all=new StringBuilder();String line;while((line=br.readLine())!=null){all.append(line).append(' ');}StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(all.toString());if(!st.hasMoreTokens())return;int T=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());StringBuilder out=new StringBuilder();while(T-- >0){int N=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());int M=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());List<Integer>[] adj=new ArrayList[N];for(int i=0;i<N;i++)adj[i]=new ArrayList<>();int[] indeg=new int[N];for(int i=0;i<M;i++){int u=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken())-1;int v=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken())-1;adj[u].add(v);indeg[v]++;}ArrayDeque<Integer> q=new ArrayDeque<>();for(int i=0;i<N;i++)if(indeg[i]==0)q.add(i);int[] order=new int[N];int idx=0;while(!q.isEmpty()){int u=q.poll();order[idx++]=u;for(int v:adj[u])if(--indeg[v]==0)q.add(v);}if(idx!=N){out.append("-1\n");}else{for(int i=0;i<N;i++){if(i>0)out.append(' ');out.append(order[i]+1);}out.append('\n');}}System.out.print(out.toString());}}
